key_insight: Physics-Informed Broad Learning System (PIBLS) solves PDEs with a backpropagation-free least-squares formulation, combining mesh-free physics constraints with a proven universal approximation property.
engineering_relevance: PIBLS offers a faster and more stable alternative to PINNs for real-time simulation, design optimization, and other PDE-driven engineering workflows.
method: Reformulate PDE solving as a direct least-squares optimization problem.
Use a broad learning system architecture instead of iterative backpropagation.
Extend the framework to nonlinear PDEs with an improved algorithm and approximation proof.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on linear and nonlinear PDEs where PIBLS is 1–3 orders of magnitude faster than conventional PINNs while achieving higher accuracy.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on problem setup and the quality of the nonlinear solver/least-squares formulation, so generalization to harder PDE classes should be validated carefully.
paper_title: Learning universal approximations for partial differential equations with Physics-Informed Broad Learning System
